narrative.executive_summary: Compute, not models, defined the week. Anthropic took the entire 300 MW Colossus 1 cluster from Elon Musk's SpaceX on May 6, gaining access to roughly 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs from a recently-merged rival and using it to immediately double Claude Code rate limits for paid tiers. The same week the company shipped Claude Opus 4.7 with a 1 million token context window at unchanged pricing and launched the Claude Platform on AWS, while OpenAI rolled out GPT-5.5 Instant as the new ChatGPT default and released three Realtime voice models. AWS made its MCP Server generally available, Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0, and Google opened the Workspace MCP server to public developer preview, marking the moment MCP stopped being an Anthropic-led protocol and became a hyperscaler standard. The composite signal is that the frontier game is now visibly capex-constrained, agent harnesses are converging on MCP plus skills as the wire format, and competitive lines are blurring fast enough that rivals are renting each other's data centers.
narrative.dominant_theme: Compute capacity displaced model quality as the binding constraint on the frontier, forcing alliances between formal rivals.
narrative.market_mood_rationale: Three frontier model releases shipped in seven days, MCP went hyperscaler-standard with AWS plus Google plus Microsoft moves, and Anthropic's willingness to rent SpaceX compute signals demand outstripping any single supplier.

biggest_move.move: Signed a deal on May 6 to take all compute capacity at SpaceX's Colossus 1 data center in Memphis — over 300 megawatts and roughly 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs — and immediately doubled Claude Code rate limits, lifted peak-hours throttles for Pro and Max, and considerably raised Opus API rate limits.
biggest_move.signal: Anthropic is willing to rent a rival's infrastructure to keep up with Claude Code demand; capex is now the binding constraint on the frontier and competitive boundaries are dissolving where compute is concerned.

narrative.competitive_frontier: The frontier shifted from model quality to compute access and protocol distribution. MCP became the cross-vendor agent standard the same week three hyperscalers shipped first-party MCP servers, and the Anthropic-SpaceX deal showed that exclusive supplier relationships no longer protect labs from each other.
narrative.open_source_signal: Trending open source concentrated almost entirely on Claude Code skills and agent harnesses — mattpocock/skills, everything-claude-code, and agency-agents all dominated the weekly leaderboard. Practitioner energy is in the skills-and-orchestration layer, not in new base models or inference engines.

strategic_takeaways.headline: Compute is the new model benchmark.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: When Anthropic rents from a SpaceX-owned rival data center to fund Claude Code rate-limit increases, the binding constraint is no longer parameter count or eval scores — it is megawatts. Capex visibility now belongs in any frontier-lab thesis.

strategic_takeaways.headline: MCP just became infrastructure.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: AWS MCP Server going GA, Google opening Workspace MCP to preview, and Microsoft shipping Agent Framework 1.0 with native MCP support in the same week means MCP has crossed from Anthropic-led protocol to cross-vendor standard. Proprietary connector marketplaces are now competing against a shared substrate.

strategic_takeaways.headline: Skills overtook agents as the unit of composition.
strategic_takeaways.explanation: The top trending repos this week are skill libraries, not new agent frameworks. The composability story is moving from agent-of-agents to skill-of-skills, which is a meaningfully smaller and more reusable abstraction.

to_watch.item: Google I/O 2026 keynote — Gemini, Gemma 4 updates, Android 17, possible managed-agent runtime on Vertex.
The Android Show I/O Edition — consumer Gemini integration ahead of the main keynote.
Whether a second frontier lab follows Anthropic in renting from a rival cluster, formalizing compute as a multi-tenant commodity.

content_angles.newsletter_angle: Frame the week as the moment the frontier became visibly capex-constrained — and explain why Anthropic renting SpaceX compute is not a partnership story, it is a supply story.
content_angles.linkedin_hook: Anthropic just rented 300 megawatts of compute from Elon Musk's SpaceX — a company that owns its largest rival — and most takes on it are missing the actual signal.

anthropic.fact: Took all compute capacity at SpaceX's Colossus 1 in Memphis — over 300 MW and roughly 220,000 NVIDIA GPUs — and doubled Claude Code rate limits across paid tiers same day.
Made Claude Opus 4.7 generally available with a 1 million token context window, improved long-running coding tasks, and higher-resolution vision; pricing unchanged at $5 in / $25 out per 1M tokens.

Launched the Claude Platform on AWS, bringing the full Claude API surface — managed agents, code execution, web tools, skills, prompt caching — with AWS-native authentication, billing, and commitment retirement.

openai.fact: Rolled out GPT-5.5 Instant as the new default ChatGPT model and as chat-latest in the API on May 5, with GPT-5.3 Instant remaining accessible to paid users for three months before retirement.

Released three Realtime voice models in the API — GPT-Realtime-2 for live voice reasoning, GPT-Realtime-Translate for real-time multilingual speech, and GPT-Realtime-Whisper for streaming transcription.

Added Analytics and Agents areas to the ChatGPT global admin console, with adoption trends, drilldowns for GPTs, projects, skills, users, tool interactions, connector interactions, and workspace health.

google.fact: Opened Workspace MCP server to public developer preview, exposing Gmail and Drive as MCP-native tools for agents.
Confirmed Google I/O 2026 dates (May 19-20) and a pre-keynote Android Show on May 12, with a dedicated Gemma session on cloud, desktop, and mobile deployment paths.

xai.fact: Confirmed compute partnership with Anthropic, renting out 300 MW of Colossus 1 capacity — the entire data center — to a rival lab; Musk publicly framed it as a commercial decision.
Launched SuperGrok Heavy with access to Grok Heavy and higher rate limits, plus deep integrations bringing daily-use apps directly into Grok.
Shipped standalone Grok Speech-to-Text and Text-to-Speech APIs with multilingual support, speaker diarization, timestamps, and expressive speech tags, plus a Quality Mode for the Grok Imagine API.

other.fact: Microsoft shipped Agent Framework 1.0 with enterprise multi-agent orchestration, multi-provider model support, and cross-runtime interoperability over A2A and MCP.

items.fact: AWS MCP Server reached general availability with IAM guardrails, CloudWatch metrics, CloudTrail logging, and sandboxed Python execution against AWS services.
Microsoft Agent Framework 1.0 added native MCP tooling, A2A interoperability, and multi-provider model routing under a single orchestration runtime.
Anthropic's Claude Platform on AWS bundled managed agents, code execution, web tools, skills, and prompt caching as first-party AWS-billable services.
Google Workspace MCP server entered public preview, giving agents Gmail and Drive as MCP tools with Workspace identity controls.

agent_capabilities.pattern: MCP became the cross-vendor agent substrate this week — three hyperscalers shipped MCP-native servers in seven days, ending the protocol's single-vendor phase.

items.fact: Claude Code shipped third-party plugin sources via --plugin-url, .zip plugin packaging with auto-installed dependencies, an agent view for multi-session management, and an official /radio command.
Claude Code Pro and Max rate limits doubled and peak-hours throttling removed following the SpaceX compute deal.
AWS Agent Toolkit bundled MCP servers, skills, and plugins targeting Claude Code, Cursor, Codex, Kiro, Cline, and Windsurf with a shared IAM-and-CloudTrail governance layer.
ChatGPT Business added an Excel and Google Sheets sidebar worldwide with Skills and apps support, free preview through 2026-06-02.

repos.description: Reusable Claude Code skills repo; spent the week as the #1 trending AI repo after peaking at six straight days of #2 trending.
Agent harness with 28 subagents, 119 skills, 60 slash commands, 14 MCP servers; crossed 100K stars in three months since Cerebral Valley hackathon.
147 specialized agents across 12 divisions with multi-tool scripts spanning Claude Code, Copilot, Gemini CLI, Cursor, Aider, Windsurf, Qwen Code, and Kimi Code.
Curated collection of 100+ specialized Claude Code subagents covering development use cases.
Intelligent automation and multi-agent orchestration for Claude Code.
Anthropic's first-party CLI coding agent; star growth held steady despite the broader market shifting attention to third-party skill repos.
